_model_version: 2.0 — Implicit diode model with R_S and power-law tunneling
_legacy_note: Reference parameters below are from the legacy TAT model (J_dark = J0·[exp(qV/AkT)-1] + V/Rsh + B·V·exp(-C/(Vbi-V))). They do NOT directly map to the current model (J_D = J01·[exp(A1·V_int)-1] + J02·[exp(A2·V_int)-1] + V_int/R_SH + k·V_int^m). Kept for historical reference.
paper_reference: Table 4.3 - 4-AMPY ligand passivation study on PbS CQD photodetectors

j_btb_exclusion.rationale: J_BTB (band-to-band tunneling) requires high doping concentrations and extremely narrow depletion regions (typically <10 nm), conditions not satisfied in PbS CQD photodetectors due to their moderate doping levels (~10^16 cm^-3) and relatively thick active layers (~200-400 nm). The CQD film's granular structure and organic ligand matrix further preclude the coherent band bending required for direct band-to-band tunneling.

self_consistency_rules._note: For the new model, physical trends remain: passivation should reduce J01/J02 (fewer traps), reduce n1/n2 toward ideality, increase R_SH, and reduce k (tunneling coefficient).

J01_J02.description: Saturation currents decrease with passivation — fewer recombination centers

n1_n2.description: Ideality factors approach n1→1, n2→2 with better passivation

R_SH.description: Shunt resistance increases — ligand passivation blocks ohmic leakage pathways

k.description: Tunneling coefficient decreases — fewer mid-gap trap states for field-assisted tunneling

R_S.description: Series resistance may decrease with improved contact quality
